data.current_description: Assign an alert to a user. Processed asynchronously; returns HTTP 202.
data.description_suggestion: Assigns an alert to a specified user. The operation is processed asynchronously and returns HTTP 202 with a request ID for tracking. The alert's assignee is updated and the user becomes responsible for the alert. Requires the alert identifier and assignee details in the request body.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks clarity on what inputs are required (alert identifier, assignee details) and what the response contains (request ID for tracking). Added details about the async nature, side effect (assignee update), and input/output expectations for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Add a responder (team or user) to an alert. Processed asynchronously; returns HTTP 202.
data.description_suggestion: Adds a responder (user or team) to an alert, expanding the alert's response group. The operation is processed asynchronously and returns HTTP 202 with a request ID for tracking. The responder is added to the alert's responder list and will receive notifications. Requires the alert identifier and responder details (type and identifier) in the request body.
data.reasoning: Original description is vague about what constitutes a responder and the side effects of adding one. Added clarity on responder types, the side effect (notification delivery), required inputs, and async response details for improved AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Add a team to an alert for visibility. Processed asynchronously; returns HTTP 202.
data.description_suggestion: Adds a team to an alert to increase visibility and ensure team members are aware of the alert. The operation is processed asynchronously and returns HTTP 202 with a request ID for tracking. The team is added to the alert's team list and team members will receive notifications. Requires the alert identifier and team identifier in the request body.
data.reasoning: Original description mentions 'visibility' but lacks specificity about what this means operationally. Added clarity on the side effects (team member notifications), required inputs, and async response details to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Escalate an alert to the next escalation policy. Processed asynchronously; returns HTTP 202.
data.description_suggestion: Escalates an alert to the next level in its escalation policy, notifying additional responders. The operation is processed asynchronously and returns HTTP 202 with a request ID for tracking. The alert's escalation level is incremented and new responders defined in the escalation policy are notified. Requires the alert identifier and optional escalation details in the request body.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks context about what escalation means operationally and what inputs/outputs are involved. Added clarity on the side effects (escalation level change, new responder notifications), required inputs, and async response details for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Snooze an alert until a specified time. Processed asynchronously; returns HTTP 202.
data.description_suggestion: Snooze an alert to temporarily suppress notifications until a specified time. Processed asynchronously and returns HTTP 202 with a request ID for status tracking.
